# DBA Common Scripts

## Opitimize

###  Index Opitimize

```mssql
/*
Index Fragmentation
if Index Fragmentation between 5 and 30 then REORGANIZE
if index Fragmentation between 30 and 100 then REBUILD
*/
SELECT DB_NAME(database_id),
	idm.object_id,
	OBJECT_NAME(idm.object_id),
	index_name = i.name,
	idm.avg_fragmentation_in_percent,
	i.index_id,
	CASE WHEN idm.avg_fragmentation_in_percent between 5 and 30
		THEN 'ALTER INDEX '+QUOTENAME(i.name)+ ' ON '+QUOTENAME(OBJECT_NAME(idm.object_id))+' REORGANIZE '
	WHEN idm.avg_fragmentation_in_percent between 30 and 100
		THEN 'ALTER INDEX '+QUOTENAME(i.name)+ ' ON '+QUOTENAME(OBJECT_NAME(idm.object_id))+' REBUILD PARTITION = ALL WITH (PAD_INDEX = OFF, STATISTICS_NORECOMPUTE = OFF, SORT_IN_TEMPDB = OFF, ONLINE = OFF, ALLOW_ROW_LOCKS = ON, ALLOW_PAGE_LOCKS = ON)'
	END as Script
FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(),null,null,null,null) idm
left join sys.indexes i
on idm.object_id = i.object_id
	and idm.index_id = i.index_id
where idm.index_depth>1
	and idm.avg_fragmentation_in_percent>5
order by idm.object_id

/*
lookup missing index
*/
SELECT
    DatabaseName = DB_NAME(database_id)
    ,[Number Indexes Missing] = count(*)
FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details
GROUP BY DB_NAME(database_id)
ORDER BY 2 DESC;

SELECT  TOP 10
        [Total Cost]  = ROUND(avg_total_user_cost * avg_user_impact * (user_seeks + user_scans),0)
        , avg_user_impact
        , TableName = statement
        , [EqualityUsage] = equality_columns
        , [InequalityUsage] = inequality_columns
        , [Include Cloumns] = included_columns
FROM        sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups g
INNER JOIN    sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats s
       ON s.group_handle = g.index_group_handle
INNER JOIN    sys.dm_db_missing_index_details d
       ON d.index_handle = g.index_handle
ORDER BY [Total Cost] DESC;

SELECT  avg_total_user_cost * avg_user_impact * ( user_scans + user_seeks ) AS PossibleImprovement ,
        last_user_seek ,
        last_user_scan ,
        [statement] AS [Object] ,
        'CREATE INDEX [IDX_' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(32), GS.group_handle) + '_'
        + CONVERT(VARCHAR(32), D.index_handle) + '_'
        + REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE([STATEMENT], ']', ''), '[', ''), '.', '')
        + ']' + ' ON ' + [statement] + ' (' + ISNULL(equality_columns, '')
        + CASE WHEN equality_columns IS NOT NULL
                    AND inequality_columns IS NOT NULL THEN ','
               ELSE ''
          END + ISNULL(inequality_columns, '') + ')' + ISNULL(' INCLUDE ('
                                                              + included_columns
                                                              + ')', '') AS Create_Index_Syntax
FROM    sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups AS G
        INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats AS GS ON G.index_group_handle = GS.group_handle
        INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_details AS D ON G.index_handle = D.index_handle
ORDER BY PossibleImprovement DESC
```

###  cpu

```mssql
/*
lookup the statement that expend the most cpu
*/
SELECT TOP 50
   total_worker_time/execution_count AS avg_cpu_cost, plan_handle,
   execution_count,
   (SELECT SUBSTRING(text, statement_start_offset/2 + 1,
      (CASE WHEN statement_end_offset = -1
         THEN LEN(CONVERT(nvarchar(max), text)) * 2
         ELSE statement_end_offset
      END - statement_start_offset)/2)
   FROM sys.dm_exec_sql_text(sql_handle)) AS query_text
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats
ORDER BY [avg_cpu_cost] DESC
```

###  Tips

```mssql
/*
Hash Index

usage:
if the column is very long for example:varchar(500)\varchar(max)\nvarchar(max)\varbinary(max)
can use hash index

Unsupported types:
the CHECKSUM function cannot be applied to non-comparable data types such as TEXT, NTEXT, IMAGE, and CURSOR.
*/
--the column url is varchar(500)
SELECT * FROM tablename WHERE URL = @URL
--add a column checksum 
alter table tablename add csURL as CHECKSUM(URL)
--create index for the column
CREATE INDEX URL_index ON tablename (csURL);
--query using index
SELECT * FROM tablename WHERE csURL =CHECKSUM(@URL)
```



## Manage

###  Shrink

````mssql
/*
the way 1 
Shrink DB LOG
*/
USE DBName
GO

sp_helpdb DBName

ALTER DATABASE DBName SET RECOVERY SIMPLE 

DBCC SHRINKFILE (2,1)

ALTER DATABASE DBName SET RECOVERY FULL 

sp_helpdb DBName

/*
the way 2
Shrink DB LOG
*/
select name,
	log_reuse_wait,
	log_reuse_wait_desc 
from sys.databases 
where log_reuse_wait>0
--if need to backup log
backup log DBName to disk = 'nul'

USE DBName
sp_helpdb DBName

DBCC SHRINKFILE(2,1)

sp_helpdb DBName

/*Shrink all databases*/
exec sp_msforeachdb 'use ? DBCC shrinkfile(2,1)'

/*Shrink TempDB File*/
DBCC SHRINKfile(N'tempdev' ,2)
````



```mssql
/*create login user for DB*/
IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name = 'scl')
BEGIN
	CREATE LOGIN [scl] WITH PASSWORD=N'salary', DEFAULT_DATABASE=[SEOContentLibrary], CHECK_EXPIRATION=OFF, CHECK_POLICY=OFF
END
GO

/*create new database*/
IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE NAME = 'DBName ')
BEGIN
	CREATE DATABASE [DBName] ON  PRIMARY 
	( NAME = N'DBName_Data', FILENAME = N'G:\sqldata\DBName_Data.mdf' , SIZE = 524288KB , FILEGROWTH = 256000KB )
	 LOG ON 
	( NAME = N'DBName_Log', FILENAME = N'L:\DBName_Log.ldf' , SIZE = 65536KB , FILEGROWTH = 102400KB )
END
GO

/*create user for specified DB*/
USE [DBName]
GO
IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sysusers WHERE name = 'scl')
BEGIN
	CREATE USER [scl] FOR LOGIN [scl] WITH DEFAULT_SCHEMA=[dbo]
END
GO

/*create role group for specified DB*/
USE [DBName]
GO
IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_principals WHERE type = 'R' AND name = 'admin')
BEGIN
	CREATE ROLE [admin] AUTHORIZATION [dbo]
END
GO
/*create role group for specified DB*/
USE [DBName]
GO
IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_principals WHERE type = 'R' AND name = 'users')
BEGIN
	CREATE ROLE [users] AUTHORIZATION [dbo]
END
GO

/*assign role group to specified user*/
GO
EXEC sp_addrolemember N'users', N'scl'
GO

/*Transaction Log Backup*/
USE [master]
GO
ALTER DATABASE DBName SET RECOVERY FULL WITH NO_WAIT
GO

USE [master]
GO

BACKUP LOG [AdventureWorks2008R2]
TO DISK = N'C:\temp\AdventureWorks2008R2_log_201711122201.trn' with compression,stats=5;
GO
BACKUP LOG [AdventureWorks2008R2]
TO DISK = N'C:\temp\AdventureWorks2008R2_log_201711122202.trn' with compression,stats=5;
GO
BACKUP LOG [AdventureWorks2008R2]
TO DISK = N'C:\temp\AdventureWorks2008R2_log_201711122203.trn' with compression,stats=5;
GO

USE [master]
GO
RESTORE HEADERONLY FROM DISK = N'C:\temp\AdventureWorks2008R2_log_201711122201.trn';
RESTORE HEADERONLY FROM DISK = N'C:\temp\AdventureWorks2008R2_log_201711122202.trn';
RESTORE HEADERONLY FROM DISK = N'C:\temp\AdventureWorks2008R2_log_201711122203.trn';


/*rename DB logic file name*/
ALTER DATABASE SEOContentLibrary MODIFY FILE(name = 'DB_data', NEWNAME = 'NewDB_data')



/*modify physical DB file path*/
ALTER DATABASE DBName
MODIFY FILE(NAME = 'DBName_Log',
FILENAME = 'L:\sqllog\DBName.ldf');

ALTER DATABASE DBName SET OFFLINE WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE

exec master..xp_cmdshell 'robocopy L:\ L:\sqllog\ DBName_Log.ldf  /Z /R:10 /W:1 /MOV /A-:SH'

ALTER DATABASE DBName SET ONLINE
GO

/*assign permission to specified object*/
GRANT SELECT,UPDATE,INSERT,DELETE ON TableName TO [Users]
GRANT SELECT,UPDATE,INSERT,DELETE ON TableName TO [admin]

GRANT EXEC ON usp_uspName TO [Users]

/*Shrink DB LOG*/
USE DBName
GO

sp_helpdb DBName

ALTER DATABASE DBName SET RECOVERY SIMPLE 

DBCC SHRINKFILE (2,1)

ALTER DATABASE DBName SET RECOVERY FULL 

sp_helpdb DBName

/*Shrink TempDB File*/
DBCC SHRINKfile(N'tempdev' ,2)


--Compressing file
EXEC master..xp_cmdshell 'C:\PROGRA~1\7-Zip\7z a -tZip \\salarybu02\DBBackup\IPAS\2019\GlobalSur
veyReport_BaseIPAS20191016_20200114051955_Full.zip \\salarybu02\DBBackup\IPAS\20
19\GlobalSurveyReport_BaseIPAS20191016_20200114051955_Full.bak'


--Find An Object On All Servers In All Databases
sp_msforeachdb 'if exists(select @@servername, "?" as DBNAME, NAME from [?].dbo.sysobjects where name = ''usp_AddCustomerTrack'')begin select @@servername, "?" as DBNAME, NAME from [?].dbo.sysobjects where name = ''usp_AddCustomerTrack'' end '
```



###  Backup

````mssql
/*view backup set(4 ways)*/
RESTORE labelonly FROM DISK = N'D:\Studio.bak'
RESTORE headeronly FROM DISK = N'D:\Studio.bak'
RESTORE filelistonly FROM DISK = N'D:\Studio.bak'
RESTORE verifyonly FROM DISK = N'D:\Studio.bak'
````



####  Full Backup Database

````mssql
/*Full Backup Database*/
USE [master]
GO
BACKUP DATABASE DBName
TO DISK = N'G:\Backup\DBName_20200114051810_Full.bak' with copy_only,checksum,compression,buffercount = 50,maxtransfersize = 4194304,stats = 5
````



####  Monitor Backup And Restore Progress In Sql Server

```mssql
--SCRIPT 1
SELECT  session_id AS SPID ,
        command ,
        a.text AS Query ,
        start_time ,
        percent_complete ,
        DATEADD(second, estimated_completion_time / 1000, GETDATE()) AS estimated_completion_time
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests r
        CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle) a
WHERE   r.command IN ( 'BACKUP DATABASE', 'RESTORE DATABASE' )
--run at 11:04
--percent complete: 63.56613
--estimated completion time: 2012-04-03 12:38:55.033
 
--SCRIPT 3
SELECT  command ,
        s.text ,
        start_time ,
        percent_complete ,
        CAST(( ( DATEDIFF(s, start_time, GETDATE()) ) / 3600 ) AS VARCHAR)
        + ' hour(s), '
        + CAST(( DATEDIFF(s, start_time, GETDATE()) % 3600 ) / 60 AS VARCHAR)
        + 'min, '
        + CAST(( DATEDIFF(s, start_time, GETDATE()) % 60 ) AS VARCHAR)
        + ' sec' AS running_time ,
        CAST(( estimated_completion_time / 3600000 ) AS VARCHAR)
        + ' hour(s), '
        + CAST(( estimated_completion_time % 3600000 ) / 60000 AS VARCHAR)
        + 'min, '
        + CAST(( estimated_completion_time % 60000 ) / 1000 AS VARCHAR)
        + ' sec' AS est_time_to_go ,
        DATEADD(second, estimated_completion_time / 1000, GETDATE()) AS est_completion_time
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests r
        CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle) s
WHERE   r.command IN ( 'RESTORE DATABASE', 'BACKUP DATABASE', 'RESTORE LOG',
                       'BACKUP LOG' )
 
--SCRIPT 3
SELECT  r.session_id ,
        r.command ,
        CONVERT(NUMERIC(6, 2), r.percent_complete) AS [Percent Complete] ,
        CONVERT(VARCHAR(20), DATEADD(ms, r.estimated_completion_time,
                                     GETDATE()), 20) AS [ETA Completion Time] ,
        CONVERT(NUMERIC(10, 2), r.total_elapsed_time / 1000.0 / 60.0) AS [Elapsed Min] ,
        CONVERT(NUMERIC(10, 2), r.estimated_completion_time / 1000.0 / 60.0) AS [ETA Min] ,
        CONVERT(NUMERIC(10, 2), r.estimated_completion_time / 1000.0 / 60.0
        / 60.0) AS [ETA Hours] ,
        CONVERT(VARCHAR(1000), ( SELECT SUBSTRING(text,
                                                  r.statement_start_offset / 2,
                                                  CASE WHEN r.statement_end_offset = -1
                                                       THEN 1000
                                                       ELSE ( r.statement_end_offset
                                                              - r.statement_start_offset )
                                                            / 2
                                                  END)
                                 FROM   sys.dm_exec_sql_text(sql_handle)
                               ))
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests r
WHERE   command IN ( 'RESTORE DATABASE', 'BACKUP DATABASE' )
 
--SCRIPT 4
SELECT  command ,
        s.text ,
        start_time ,
        percent_complete ,
        CAST(( ( DATEDIFF(s, start_time, GETDATE()) ) / 3600 ) AS VARCHAR)
        + ' hour(s), '
        + CAST(( DATEDIFF(s, start_time, GETDATE()) % 3600 ) / 60 AS VARCHAR)
        + 'min, '
        + CAST(( DATEDIFF(s, start_time, GETDATE()) % 60 ) AS VARCHAR)
        + ' sec' AS running_time ,
        CAST(( estimated_completion_time / 3600000 ) AS VARCHAR)
        + ' hour(s), '
        + CAST(( estimated_completion_time % 3600000 ) / 60000 AS VARCHAR)
        + 'min, '
        + CAST(( estimated_completion_time % 60000 ) / 1000 AS VARCHAR)
        + ' sec' AS est_time_to_go ,
        DATEADD(second, estimated_completion_time / 1000, GETDATE()) AS est_completion_time
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests r
        CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle) s
WHERE   r.command IN ( 'RESTORE DATABASE', 'BACKUP DATABASE', 'RESTORE LOG',
                       'BACKUP LOG' )
 
--SCRIPT 5
SELECT  dmr.session_id ,
        dmr.status ,
        dmr.start_time ,
        dmr.command ,
        dmt.TEXT ,
        dmr.percent_complete
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests dmr
        CROSS APPLY sys.Dm_exec_sql_text(dmr.sql_handle) dmt
WHERE   dmr.command IN ( 'ALTER', 'Backup', 'Restore', 'DBCC', 'Rollback',
                         'TDE' )
 
--SCRIPT 6
SELECT  percent_complete ,
        ( estimated_completion_time / 1000 ) / 60 Estimated_completion_time_Mins ,
        ( total_elapsed_time / 1000 ) / 60 Total_Elapsed_Time_Mins ,
        DB_NAME(Database_id) DBName ,
        *
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests
WHERE   session_id = 129
 
--SCRIPT 7
USE master
SELECT  session_id AS [SPID] ,
        CONVERT(VARCHAR(50), start_time, 120) AS [StartTime] ,
        percent_complete AS [PercentageComplete] ,
        CONVERT(VARCHAR(50), DATEADD(second, estimated_completion_time / 1000,
                                     GETDATE()), 120) AS [EstimatedCompletionTime] ,
        command AS [Command] ,
        a.text AS [OriginalQuery]
FROM    sys.dm_exec_requests r
        CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle) a
WHERE   r.command LIKE 'BACKUP%'
        OR r.command LIKE 'RESTORE%'
```

####  Restore Database

````mssql
/*Restore Database*/
USE [master]

ALTER DATABASE TestDB20190515 SET SINGLE_USER WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE;
RESTORE DATABASE TestDB20190515
FROM DISK = N'D:\Backup\TestDB_backup_201905150110.bak' WITH FILE=1,Replace,stats = 10,
MOVE 'TestDB_Data' TO N'G:\sqldata\TestDB_Data_20190515.mdf',
MOVE 'TestDB_Log' TO N'G:\sqldata\TestDB_Log_20190515.ldf'

/***************************************************
Set DB owner to SA
****************************************************/

alter authorization on database::TestDB20190515 to sa

EXEC sp_change_users_login 'Update_One','jbd','jbd'
/*mapping user*/

create table #temp1(idx int identity,UserName sysname,UserSid varbinary(85))
insert #temp1(UserName,UserSid) exec sp_change_users_login 'report'
declare @UserName varchar(100)
declare @s varchar(1000)
declare c cursor for
	select username from #temp1
open c
fetch next from c into @UserName
while @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
begin
      if exists (select * from master.sys.syslogins where name=@UserName)
         begin
            set @s='sp_change_users_login ''Update_One'','''+@UserName+''','''+@UserName+''''
            print (@s)
            exec (@s)
         end
         fetch next from c into @UserName

 end
close c
deallocate c
````



###  Snapshot

````mssql
/*Snapshot*/
use master

CREATE DATABASE Snapshot_GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609 ON(
	NAME = N'GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609_Data',
	FILENAME = N'G:\sqldata\Snapshot_GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609_data.mdf' -- a new physical name
) AS
SNAPSHOT OF GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609

--drop snapshot
DROP DATABASE Snapshot_GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609

--restore database from snapshot
ALTER DATABASE GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609 SET SINGLE_USER WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE;
RESTORE DATABASE GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609 FROM DATABASE_SNAPSHOT = 'Snapshot_GlobalSurveyReport_BaseGBS20200609'
````

###  CMD Commmand

````mssql
/*CMD Commmand*/

--move
EXEC master..xp_cmdshell 'move /y D:\movies.txt D:\Test\movies.txt'

--copy
EXEC master..xp_cmdshell 'copy /y D:\Test\TestCopy\*.txt D:\Test\TestCopy\combined.doc'

EXEC master..xp_cmdshell 'copy /y D:\Test\Testsql\*.sql D:\Test\Testsql\combined.sql'

--create folder

select @cmd = 'exec master..xp_cmdshell "if not exist '+@backupdir+ ' md '+@backupdir+'"'
--select @cmd
exec (@CMD)

--robocopy
exec master..xp_cmdshell 'robocopy D:\Test\ D:\Test\Testsql\ movies.txt /Z /R:10 /W:1 /MOV /A-:SH'
````





















## T-Sql

```mssql
--cursor 1
Declare @c cursor local static read_only forward_only

SET @c = CURSOR FOR
	SELECT xx FROM tbl_table

OPEN @c
FETCH NEXT FROM @c INTO @XX
WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
BEGIN
	.....

	FETCH NEXT FROM @c INTO @XX
END
CLOSE @c
DEALLOCATE @c

--cursor 2
Declare c cursor local static read_only forward_only
FOR
	SELECT xx FROM tbl_table

OPEN c
FETCH NEXT FROM c INTO @XX
WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
BEGIN
	.....

	FETCH NEXT FROM c INTO @XX
END
CLOSE c
DEALLOCATE c

/*sqlcmd*/
SET DATADBSERVER=salsql03
SET RUNCODE=BaseIPAS20200404
sqlcmd -S %DATADBSERVER% -w 2000 -r -h-1 -E -v RUNCODE=%RUNCODE% -i _GetTargetList.sql -o _TargetList.txt 2>error_targetlist.log

/*sql Encryption*/
--database master key
CREATE MASTER KEY ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD ='Salary.com'


--CERTIFICATE
CREATE CERTIFICATE CertTest 
with SUBJECT = 'Test Certificate'
GO
--ASYMMETRIC
CREATE ASYMMETRIC KEY TestAsymmetric
    WITH ALGORITHM = RSA_2048 
    ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'Salary.com'; 
GO
--SYMMETRIC
CREATE SYMMETRIC KEY TestSymmetric
    WITH ALGORITHM = AES_256
    ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'Salary.com';
GO

--create SYMMETRIC KEY by CERTIFICATE
CREATE SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricByCert
    WITH ALGORITHM = AES_256
    ENCRYPTION BY CERTIFICATE CertTest;
GO

-- OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY BY SYMMETRIC KEY
OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY TestSymmetric
    DECRYPTION BY PASSWORD='Salary.com'

--create SYMMETRIC by SYMMETRIC
CREATE SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricBySy
    WITH ALGORITHM = AES_256
    ENCRYPTION BY SYMMETRIC KEY TestSymmetric;
GO

--CREATE SYMMETRIC by ASYMMETRIC
CREATE SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricByAsy
    WITH ALGORITHM = AES_256
    ENCRYPTION BY ASYMMETRIC KEY TestASymmetric;
GO
--drop table tbl_UserCard
CREATE TABLE tbl_UserCard(
	CreditCardID int not null,
	CardNumber varbinary(500) not null
)


OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricByCert
DECRYPTION BY CERTIFICATE CertTest

insert into tbl_UserCard
select 1,Encryptbykey(KEY_GUID('SymmetricByCert'),'33332664695310')
Close SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricByCert
--values(1,'33332664695310'),(2,'55552127249722')

OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricByCert
DECRYPTION BY CERTIFICATE CertTest

select CreditCardID,
	convert(varchar(32),DECRYPTBYKEY(CardNumber))
from tbl_UserCard

Close SYMMETRIC KEY SymmetricByCert

SELECT ENCRYPTBYPASSPHRASE('Salary.com','20200819',0) hirdate into #EncryTest

select convert(varchar(32),DECRYPTBYPASSPHRASE('Salary.com',hirdate,0)) from #EncryTest


select HASHBYTES('sha1','123456a')
```



##  CLR

```mssql
ALTER DATABASE master SET TRUSTWORTHY ON
go

CREATE ASSEMBLY [Database2]
FROM 
WITH PERMISSION_SET = UNSAFE
GO

/*

--报错
    消息 10327，级别 14，状态 1，第 1 行
针对程序集 'Database2' 的 CREATE ASSEMBLY 失败，因为程序集 'Database2' 未获授权，
不满足 PERMISSION_SET = UNSAFE。满足以下两个条件之一时将给程序集授权: 
数据库所有者(DBO)拥有 UNSAFE ASSEMBLY 权限，且数据库具有 TRUSTWORTHY 数据库属性；
或者，程序集已使用其对应登录名具有 UNSAFE ASSEMBLY 权限的证书或非对称密钥加以签名。

--解决
    ALTER DATABASE master SET TRUSTWORTHY ON
    go
*/


exec sp_configure 'show advanced options',1 
reconfigure 
exec sp_configure 'clr enabled',1 
reconfigure 
exec sp_configure 'show advanced options',0 
RECONFIGURE
go







/*
DROP AGGREGATE  String_Agg
DROP FUNCTION Capit
DROP FUNCTION Fn_Cacl
DROP FUNCTION FormatDateTime
DROP FUNCTION MyRound 
DROP FUNCTION MyTrim
DROP FUNCTION PadLeft
DROP FUNCTION PadRight
DROP FUNCTION Reg_CharIndex 
DROP FUNCTION Reg_Match 
DROP FUNCTION Reg_Replace 
DROP FUNCTION ToDBC
DROP FUNCTION ToSBC
DROP FUNCTION TrimAll
DROP FUNCTION TrimEnd
DROP FUNCTION TrimStart
DROP FUNCTION Ft_GetNumbers
DROP FUNCTION Reg_Split
DROP FUNCTION String_Split
DROP PROCEDURE  BulkCopyToXls
DROP PROCEDURE sp_getman
DROP PROCEDURE sp_postman
DROP PROCEDURE sp_ReadTxt
DROP FUNCTION CharAt
DROP FUNCTION ft_clearing
DROP ASSEMBLY database2 
*/
--CLR

CREATE AGGREGATE [dbo].[String_Agg](@value NVARCHAR (4000))
    RETURNS NVARCHAR (4000)
    EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[String_Agg]
go

CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Capit]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Capit]
 
GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Fn_Cacl]
(@exp NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (1000)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Fn_Cacl]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[FormatDateTime]
(@input DATETIME, @fmt NVARCHAR (200))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (200)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[FormatDateTime]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[MyRound]
(@val FLOAT (53), @dec SMALLINT)
RETURNS FLOAT (53)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[MyRound]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[MyTrim]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[MyTrim]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[PadLeft]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @i INT, @c NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[PadLeft]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[PadRight]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @i INT, @c NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[PadRight]
 
GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Reg_CharIndex]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @sp NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS INT
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Reg_CharIndex]
 
GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Reg_Match]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @sp NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Reg_Match]
 
GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Reg_Replace]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @sp NVARCHAR (1000), @x NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Reg_Replace]
Go

AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[ft_clearing]

*/

(@input NVARCHAR (MAX))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[ToDBC]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[ToSBC]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[ToSBC]


GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[TrimAll]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @chars NVARCHAR (100))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[TrimAll]


GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[TrimEnd]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @chars NVARCHAR (100))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[TrimEnd]
 
GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[TrimStart]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @chars NVARCHAR (100))
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[TrimStart]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Ft_GetNumbers]
(@n INT)
RETURNS 
     TABLE (
        [Val] INT NULL)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Ft_GetNumbers]


GO
 
 
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[Reg_Split]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @sp NVARCHAR (1000))
RETURNS 
     TABLE (
        [id] INT, 
        [Val] NVARCHAR (4000) NULL)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[Reg_Split]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[String_Split]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @sp NVARCHAR (1))
RETURNS 
     TABLE (
        [id] INT,
        [Val] NVARCHAR (4000) NULL)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[String_Split]
 
GO
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[BulkCopyToXls]
@sql NVARCHAR (4000), @savePath NVARCHAR (1000), @tableName NVARCHAR (200), @maxRecordCount INT
AS EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[myProcedure].[BulkCopyToXls]


GO
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[sp_getman]
@url NVARCHAR (1000), @result NVARCHAR (MAX) OUTPUT
AS EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[StoredProcedures].[sp_getman]


GO
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[sp_postman]
@url NVARCHAR (1000), @json NVARCHAR (4000), @result NVARCHAR (MAX) OUTPUT
AS EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[StoredProcedures].[sp_postman]


GO
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[sp_ReadTxt]
@file NVARCHAR (1000), @coding NVARCHAR (100)
AS EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[StoredProcedures].[sp_ReadTxt]

GO
CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[CharAt]
(@input NVARCHAR (MAX), @chars NVARCHAR (100),@i int)
RETURNS NVARCHAR (MAX)
AS
 EXTERNAL NAME [Database2].[UserDefinedFunctions].[CharAt]
```
